copy.title: Knicks steal Game 1 from Spurs. Nvidia and Microsoft tank hard. Monaco Grand Prix this weekend.
copy.keyTakeaway: Knicks beat Spurs 105-95 in Finals Game 1 behind strong defense. Tech stocks dropped across the board. CBS News continues imploding after Pelley firing.

todaysHits.sports: Knicks steal Game 1 in San Antonio. Spurs can't find rhythm.
todaysHits.markets: Tech slides. SPY down 0.7%, NVDA -3.6%, MSFT -3.2%.
todaysHits.golf: Campbell leads Memorial Tournament at even par.
todaysHits.f1: Monaco Grand Prix arrives this weekend.
todaysHits.worldcup: World Cup 2026 kicks off June 11. Zero matches played.
todaysHits.culture: CBS News chaos deepens. 60 Minutes loses Pelley. Summer House explodes.

lead.headline: Brunson leads Knicks past Wembanyama's Spurs in Finals Game 1
lead.whatHappened: Jalen Brunson dropped 30 points to lift New York past San Antonio 105–95 in the NBA Finals opener. The Knicks' offense ran through Brunson all night while Victor Wembanyama countered with 26 points and 12 rebounds, but it wasn't enough to keep pace.
lead.whyBullet1: New York's depth showed up—Karl-Anthony Towns added 18 and 12 boards, OG Anunoby chipped in 17—while San Antonio's supporting cast around Wembanyama couldn't generate enough offense to match.
lead.whyBullet2: Brunson's ability to control the pace and create for others (only 2 assists though) suggests the Knicks' guard play will be the deciding factor in this series.
lead.whatToSay: Brunson's 30 was efficient and he looked in control the whole way. Wembanyama's a problem on the glass and defensively, but the Knicks have too many scorers for one guy to cover. Game 2 tomorrow in San Antonio gets interesting if the Spurs can tighten up their bench scoring.

markets.mood: Tech stocks sold off hard with SPY down 0.7% and the Nasdaq basically flat, but the real story is the 10-year yield jumping 0.8% in a single day — something spooked the bond market.
markets.whyBullet1: Big tech names like Microsoft and Nvidia both dropped 3%+ while Treasury yields spiked, which means traders are either pricing in higher rates for longer or rotating out of growth stocks into fixed income — both signal concern about inflation staying stubborn.
markets.whyBullet2: The market's waiting on Friday's jobs report to see if wage growth is still accelerating, because that's what's been keeping the Fed from cutting rates and pushing yields higher.
markets.bringUp: Bitcoin dropped 7.3% today alone, which is a bigger move than the stock market — crypto traders are treating rising Treasury yields as a headwind since bonds now compete harder for investor cash.

golf.headline: Campbell, Coody, Fox tied at Memorial; elite field tests Muirfield Village
golf.whyCare1: Memorial is a major-prep event where the course's difficulty separates pretenders from actual players headed into summer majors.
golf.whyCare2: Muirfield Village rewards precision over power—three guys tied means someone's about to separate in a tournament that punishes mistakes.
golf.watchFor: Whether Campbell can hold the lead; he's been under the radar all season and this is a statement moment.
golf.whatToSay: Memorial's the kind of course where you can't just bomb it—that's why you see the real players rise this week.

culture.topic: Scott Pelley Out at CBS News 60 Minutes
culture.whatHappened: CBS News fired Scott Pelley from 60 Minutes, the latest executive shakeup at a network already reeling from internal chaos.
culture.whyItMatters: When flagship news operations start bleeding talent and credibility, it signals deeper rot than just one bad hire or one bad decision.
culture.whatToSay: CBS News is a mess right now. Pelley was one of the last guys actually holding it together.

culture.topic: Garrett Crochet Sidelined With Lat Strain
culture.whatHappened: White Sox pitcher Garrett Crochet got diagnosed with a low-grade lat strain, putting him on the shelf during a critical stretch.
culture.whyItMatters: You lose your ace mid-season and your rotation falls apart. The Sox already have enough problems without betting everything on a pitcher rehabbing.
culture.whatToSay: Crochet going down is bad timing for Chicago. They can't afford to lose innings right now.

culture.whatHappened: Billy Bob Thornton running Texas oil country — part business thriller, part character study about men who operate by a code that predates HR departments.

copy.finalSharpTake: The Knicks won Game 1 at home by ten, which is fine but not dominant—San Antonio kept it close enough to matter. Tomorrow in Austin is where we'll actually learn something. Tech stocks got hit hard today, down 3 percent across the board, but that's noise compared to what matters: the bond market moved, yields up nearly a point, which tells you the real money is repositioning. Everything else is just reaction.
